Cereus polygonus, a cactus species, is a succulent plant native to the Americas, characterized by its columnar stem and sharp spines. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Cereus polygonus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which may contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cereus polygonus is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
